tree.setup: You're trying to reach the delivery company about a package that hasn't arrived. After navigating a phone menu, you finally reach a human -- Xiao Zhu, a customer service rep who sounds like she's smiling even through the phone. She has a way of making logistics sound almost cheerful.
tree.setup_zh: 你在打快递公司的电话，问一个没到的包裹。听了一圈语音菜单之后，终于接通了人——客服小朱，听声音好像在笑。她有本事把物流的事说得很开心。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 您好，快递客服。请问您的快递单号是多少？
turns.text_pinyin: nín hǎo, kuàidì kèfú. qǐngwèn nín de kuàidì dān hào shì duōshao?
turns.text_en: Hello, delivery service. What's your tracking number?

options.text_zh: 单号是SF123456。我的快递还没到。
options.text_pinyin: dān hào shì SF yāo èr sān sì wǔ liù. wǒ de kuàidì hái méi dào.
options.text_en: The tracking number is SF123456. My delivery hasn't arrived yet.
options.feedback: Xiao Zhu types it in.「我查一下……在路上了！今天下午应该到。」还没到 means 'hasn't arrived yet' -- 还没 + verb is the 'not yet' pattern.

options.text_zh: 我等了三天了！我的东西在哪里？
options.text_pinyin: wǒ děng le sān tiān le! wǒ de dōngxi zài nǎlǐ?
options.text_en: I've been waiting three days! Where's my stuff?
options.feedback: Xiao Zhu stays cheerful.「理解理解！您告诉我单号，我马上帮您查。」等了三天了 uses the double 了 to emphasize ongoing duration. Effective, if slightly intense.

options.text_zh: 我没有单号。可以用手机号查吗？
options.text_pinyin: wǒ méiyǒu dān hào. kěyǐ yòng shǒujī hào chá ma?
options.text_en: I don't have the tracking number. Can I look it up by phone number?
options.feedback: Xiao Zhu says yes without missing a beat.「可以！请告诉我您的手机号。」Knowing alternative ways to look things up is practical wisdom.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 查到了！您的快递在路上了，今天下午到。如果不在家，快递员会打电话给您。
turns.text_pinyin: chá dào le! nín de kuàidì zài lùshang le, jīntiān xiàwǔ dào. rúguǒ bú zài jiā, kuàidìyuán huì dǎ diànhuà gěi nín.
turns.text_en: Found it! Your delivery is on its way, arriving this afternoon. If you're not home, the courier will call you.

options.text_zh: 好的，谢谢！可以放在门口吗？
options.text_pinyin: hǎo de, xièxie! kěyǐ fàng zài ménkǒu ma?
options.text_en: OK, thanks! Can they leave it at the door?
options.feedback: Xiao Zhu notes it.「好的，我备注一下。」放在门口 is how millions of Chinese packages get delivered every day.

options.text_zh: 我下午不在家。明天可以送吗？
options.text_pinyin: wǒ xiàwǔ bú zài jiā. míngtiān kěyǐ sòng ma?
options.text_en: I won't be home this afternoon. Can they deliver tomorrow?
options.feedback: Xiao Zhu arranges it happily.「没问题，明天上午给您送。」Rescheduling with 可以...吗 is clean and polite.
Xiao Zhu's smile is audible.「还有别的事吗？」Short and satisfied. Sometimes that's all you need.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好的，还有别的事吗？祝您生活愉快！
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo de, hái yǒu bié de shì ma? zhù nín shēnghuó yúkuài!
turns.text_en: OK, anything else? Wishing you a pleasant day!

tree.cultural_note: China's delivery system (快递, kuàidì) is astonishingly fast -- same-city packages often arrive within hours. Couriers (快递员) will call before delivering. Leaving packages at the door or in lockers (快递柜) is extremely common.
